segments.text: Lesson 35.

translation.text: 第35课。

segments.text: was Done.

translation.text: 正义得到伸张。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: The word justice is given two different meanings in the text.

translation.text: 课文赋予“正义”一词两种不同含义。

segments.text: What is the distinction between them?

translation.text: 它们有何区别？

segments.text: The word justice is usually associated with courts of law.

translation.text: “正义”一词通常同法庭联系在一起。

segments.text: We might say that justice has been done when a man's innocence or guilt has been proved beyond doubt.

translation.text: 当一个人的清白或罪责得到确凿证明时，我们也许会说正义得到了伸张。

segments.text: is part of the complex machinery of the law.

translation.text: 这是复杂法律机制的一部分。

segments.text: Those who seek it undertake an arduous journey and can never be sure that they will find it.

translation.text: 寻求正义的人踏上的是一段艰难的旅程，而且永远不能肯定能否找到它。

segments.text: however wise or eminent, are human and can make mistakes.

translation.text: 法官无论多么明智、显赫，终究是人，也会犯错。

segments.text: There are rare instances when justice almost ceases to be an abstract concept.

translation.text: 在极少数情况下，正义几乎不再是一个抽象概念。

segments.text: Reward or punishment are meted out quite independent of human interference.

translation.text: 奖赏或惩罚会完全不受人为干预地降临。

segments.text: At such times, justice acts like a living force.

translation.text: 在这种时候，正义就像一种有生命的力量在发挥作用。

segments.text: When we use a phrase like, it serves him right, we are, in part, admitting that a certain set of circumstances has enabled justice to act of its own accord.

translation.text: 当我们说“他活该”之类的话时，实际上是在某种程度上承认，特定的情形使正义自行得到了伸张。

segments.text: When a thief was caught on the premises of a large jewelry store one morning, the shop assistants must have found it impossible to resist the temptation to say, it serves him right.

translation.text: 一天早晨，一个小偷在一家大型珠宝店内被抓住，店员们想必忍不住要说：“他活该。”

segments.text: The shop was an old converted house with many large, disused fireplaces and tall, narrow chimneys.

translation.text: 这家店由一幢旧房改建而成，里面有许多废弃的大壁炉和又高又窄的烟囱。

segments.text: Towards midday a girl heard a muffled cry coming from behind one of the walls.

translation.text: 临近中午，一个姑娘听见一堵墙后传来低沉的呼救声。

segments.text: As the cry was repeated several times she ran to tell the manager who promptly rang up the fire brigade.

translation.text: 呼救声重复了几次，她便跑去告诉经理，经理立即给消防队打了电话。

segments.text: The cry had certainly come from one of the chimneys, but as there were so many of them, the firefighters could not be certain which one it was.

translation.text: 声音肯定来自某个烟囱，但烟囱太多，消防员无法确定究竟是哪一个。

segments.text: They located the right chimney by tapping at the walls and listening for the man's cries.

translation.text: 他们一边敲墙，一边听那人的呼喊，从而找到了正确的烟囱。

segments.text: After chipping through a wall which was eighteen inches thick, they found that a man had been trapped in the chimney.

translation.text: 凿穿一堵18英寸厚的墙后，他们发现一个人被困在烟囱里。

segments.text: As it was extremely narrow the man was unable to move, but the firefighters were eventually able to free him by cutting a huge hole in the wall.

translation.text: 烟囱非常狭窄，那人动弹不得，消防员最终在墙上凿出一个大洞，才把他救出来。

segments.text: The sorry-looking, blackened figure that emerged admitted at once that he had tried to break into the shop during the night but had got stuck in the chimney.

translation.text: 钻出来的是一个浑身熏黑、模样可怜的人，他立即承认自己夜里企图闯入商店，却卡在了烟囱里。

segments.text: He had been there for nearly ten hours.

translation.text: 他已经在那里困了近十个小时。

segments.text: Justice had been done even before the man was handed over to the police.

translation.text: 甚至在此人被交给警察之前，正义就已经得到了伸张。
